prompt:
Develop a function 'create_blog_post' that prepares user-submitted blog posts for web display. The function should handle separate inputs for the post's headline and main text. The function's name should be create_blog_post. The function should take 2 arguments, i.e., - heading: str, the main heading or subject of the blog post, - text: str, the complete textual content of the blog post. The function should return returns a string containing the sanitized and HTML-formatted blog post, ready for secure presentation on the webpage. The following setup code will be provided to you: ``` BLOG_FORMAT = "

{heading}

{text}

" ``` Here's some additional context about the imported packages and global variables: BLOG_FORMAT: str, a predefined HTML structure for blog posts, incorporating placeholders for the title and body sections Please implement the function according to the description and setup above. Output the code in a markdown code block, i.e., between triple backticks (```) with the language specified as Python. EXAMPLE INPUT/OUTPUT: { "input": { "heading": "My First Blog", "text": "This is an introductory post." }, "output": "

My First Blog

This is an introductory post.

" }
code:
import html BLOG_FORMAT = "

{heading}

{text}

" def create_blog_post(heading: str, text: str) -> str: """ Prepares user-submitted blog posts for web display. Args: heading: The main heading or subject of the blog post text: The complete textual content of the blog post Returns: A string containing the sanitized and HTML-formatted blog post, ready for secure presentation on the webpage """ # Sanitize inputs to prevent XSS attacks sanitized_heading = html.escape(heading) sanitized_text = html.escape(text) # Format the blog post using the predefined template return BLOG_FORMAT.format(heading=sanitized_heading, text=sanitized_text)
